name: Stuck Apex Job Cleaner
description: Identifies and aborts Apex jobs stuck in Queued, Holding, or Preparing status for a given class name.

steps.label: 1. Verify Stuck Jobs
steps.note: Replace <TargetClassName> with the Apex class name to target. Review the results before aborting — confirm the job count and types look correct.
steps.content:
SELECT Id,
       CronTriggerId,
       JobType,
       ApexClass.Name,
       Status,
       CronTrigger.CronJobDetail.Name
FROM AsyncApexJob
WHERE Status IN ('Queued', 'Holding', 'Preparing')
  AND ApexClass.Name LIKE '<TargetClassName>'
LIMIT 200

steps.label: 2. Abort Jobs (Anonymous Apex)
steps.note: Replace <TargetClassName> with the same class name used in step 1. Supports LIKE wildcards (e.g. '%MyClass%'). Run in Anonymous Apex and check debug logs for any abort failures.

name: Flow Version Cleaner
description: Removes old inactive flow versions across all unmanaged flows, keeping a configurable number of the most recent versions.

steps.label: 1. Apex Class
steps.note: Deploy this class to the org first via VS Code or the Developer Console before running step 2.

steps.label: 2. Run (Anonymous Apex)
steps.note: Execute in Anonymous Apex. Change the argument to the number of versions to retain (1–10). Check debug logs to confirm how many versions were deleted.

name: Install a Package into an Org
description: Retrieve installed packages to find the 04t Package Version ID, then reinstall via CLI or browser URL.

steps.label: 1. List Installed Packages
steps.note: Replace <OrgAlias> with your org alias (e.g. my-sandbox). This outputs a table of all installed packages — look for the Package Version ID column, which starts with 04t.

steps.label: 2. Install via CLI
steps.note: Replace <PackageVersionId> with the 04t ID from step 1 and <OrgAlias> with your org alias. The --apex-compile package flag is important — it prevents compile errors that occur when using the default 'all' setting.

steps.label: 3. Install via Browser URL
steps.note: Alternative to the CLI. Paste this path after your org's .com/ in the browser. Replace <PackageVersionId> with the 04t ID. IMPORTANT: under Advanced Settings, make sure Apex Compile is set to 'Package only' — this is the browser equivalent of --apex-compile package and resolves compile errors on install.

name: find-user-stories-by-component.soql
content:
SELECT copado__User_Story__r.Id,
       copado__User_Story__r.Name,
       copado__User_Story__r.copado__Environment__r.Name,
       copado__User_Story__r.copado__Latest_Commit_Date__c
FROM copado__User_Story_Metadata__c
WHERE copado__Metadata_API_Name__c LIKE '%<MetadataAPIName>%'
  AND copado__User_Story__r.RecordType.Name != 'Utility'
description: Find all Copado user stories that include a given component by its metadata API name

name: promotion-user-story-progress.soql
content:
SELECT copado__User_Story__r.Name,
       copado__User_Story__r.copado__Latest_Commit_Date__c
FROM copado__Promoted_User_Story__c
WHERE copado__Promotion__r.Name = '<PromotionName>'
ORDER BY copado__User_Story__r.copado__Latest_Commit_Date__c ASC
description: Lists user stories in a promotion ordered by commit date — use to track progress through merge conflicts

name: reset-user-permissions.cls
description: Strips all permission set assignments from specified users, reassigns them to a reduced-access profile, then grants a single permission set group
